method: Browser navigation and layout measurements only. The browser selected the no-analytics preference so the consent dialog would not mask keyboard testing; no form, subscription, authentication, or production action was invoked.

directory_fixture_listing.evidence: The directory rendered the search controls and both populated synthetic fixture listings.

directory_mobile_overflow.evidence: At every requested viewport, the document and body scroll widths equaled the client width; no horizontal page overflow was present.

keyboard_skip_link.evidence: The automated browser harness located the unique Skip to content link and sent Enter, but neither the URL fragment nor active-element target changed. This is not enough to distinguish a harness limitation from an application defect; manual keyboard verification remains required.

conclusion: This strengthens the staging mobile layout evidence but does not close the full customer-journey gate. Manual keyboard, screen-reader, touch, 200% zoom, constrained-network, error, subscription, and payment journeys remain required.
